page: Vultr vbm-4c-32gb is a dedicated bare metal server belonging to the Bare Metal SSD family. It features an Intel Xeon 1270 processor built on the x86_64 architecture, offering 4 physical cores and 8 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 3.8 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 32.0 GB of system memory, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core, alongside 480 GB of local SSD storage. No GPU resources are included on this platform.

Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities across most standard workloads. The single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests yield scores of 1728.79 and 8815.11 respectively, placing them in the average tier. Single-threaded gzip compression is a notable strength at 30.56 GB/sec. However, memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks is weak, with read speeds at 31.11 GB/sec and write speeds at 14.73 GB/sec. Large LLM inference is also a weak point, with a 7B model achieving only 6.87 tokens/sec for text generation.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this bare metal server offers dedicated, non-virtualized hardware resources with a balanced memory-to-core ratio, making it a stable option for workloads requiring consistent performance without hypervisor overhead. It is well-suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving with a throughput of 7.05 GB/sec, Redis caching handling 8.08 million SET operations per second, and moderate database operations.
description: The Vultr vbm-4c-32gb is an x86_64 bare metal server in the Bare Metal SSD family, powered by an Intel Xeon 1270 processor with 4 physical cores, 8 vCPUs, and a 3.8 GHz clock speed. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM and 480 GB of local SSD storage, with no GPU acceleration. Benchmark results show average performance across most compute and database operations, highlighted by a strong single-threaded gzip compression speed of 30.56 GB/sec and a Passmark memory score in the top tier. Conversely, uncached large-block memory bandwidth and medium-sized LLM inference speeds represent performance tradeoffs. This server is suitable for general-purpose computing, static web serving, Redis caching, and moderate database workloads requiring dedicated hardware.
og_description: Vultr vbm-4c-32gb is an x86_64 bare metal server featuring an Intel Xeon 1270 CPU with 4 physical cores, 32 GB RAM, and 480 GB SSD storage, designed for general-purpose workloads and static web serving.
meta_description: Vultr vbm-4c-32gb is an Intel Xeon bare metal server with 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 480 GB SSD storage for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A dedicated bare metal server featuring an eight-thread Intel Xeon processor, balanced memory, and local SSD storage for consistent general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 1270 processor with 4 physical cores and 8 vCPUs at 3.8 GHz
32 GB of system memory and 480 GB of local SSD storage
Strong single-threaded gzip compression performance at 30.56 GB/sec
Dedicated bare metal resources without virtualization overhead
Best suited for static web serving, Redis caching, and general-purpose applications
